title: InReverseOrder not used when ORDER BY includes constant value (and under RLS)
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the situation
When retrieving the most recent rows from a table sorted by (tenant, timestamp, …), the reverse-order read optimization is applied if the tenant constraint is written explicitly, but it is not applied when the ORDER BY includes the tenant key even though the tenant value is constant. The same optimization also isn’t triggered when the tenant constraint is enforced via row-level security rather than an explicit predicate, leading to unexpectedly slow “latest-N” queries.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
latest
### How to reproduce
```
DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS repro;
CREATE DATABASE repro;
CREATE TABLE repro.events
(
tenant String,
event_time DateTime,
id UInt64,
msg String
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
PARTITION BY toYYYYMMDD(event_time)
ORDER BY (tenant, event_time, id);
INSERT INTO repro.events
SELECT
toString(number % 100) AS tenant,
now() - (number % 200000) AS event_time,
number AS id,
concat('m', toString(number)) AS msg
FROM numbers(2000000);
-- RLS setup
DROP USER IF EXISTS rls_user;
CREATE USER rls_user IDENTIFIED WITH sha256_password BY 'x' HOST ANY;
GRANT SELECT ON repro.* TO rls_user;
DROP ROW POLICY IF EXISTS tenant_rls ON repro.events;
CREATE ROW POLICY tenant_rls
ON repro.events
FOR SELECT
USING tenant = '42'
TO rls_user;
```
With RLS user:
```
-- ReadType: default (slow)
EXPLAIN PLAN actions=1
SELECT *
FROM repro.events
ORDER BY event_time DESC
LIMIT 5;
-- ReadType: InReverseOrder (fast!)
EXPLAIN PLAN actions=1
SELECT *
FROM repro.events
poc:
CREATE TABLE repro.events
(
    tenant String,
    event_time DateTime,
    id UInt64,
    msg String
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
PARTITION BY toYYYYMMDD(event_time)
ORDER BY (tenant, event_time, id);

INSERT INTO repro.events
SELECT
    toString(number % 100) AS tenant,
    now() - (number % 200000) AS event_time,
    number AS id,
    concat('m', toString(number)) AS msg
FROM numbers(2000000);
key_bug_features: ORDER BY includes constant value with row-level security
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE repro.events (tenant String, event_time DateTime, id UInt64, msg String, value Nullable(Float64)) ENGINE = MergeTree PARTITION BY toYYYYMMDD(event_time) ORDER BY (tenant, event_time, id);

INSERT INTO repro.events SELECT toString(number % 100) AS tenant, now() - (number % 200000) AS event_time, number AS id, concat('m', toString(number)) AS msg, CAST(number % 1000 AS Nullable(Float64)) AS value FROM numbers(2000000);
SELECT * FROM repro.events WHERE value IS NULL ORDER BY event_time DESC LIMIT 5;
